nodes.name: Standing
nodes.description: Both athletes on their feet. Starting state for most matches and target for technical standups.

nodes.name: Closed Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player's legs locked around opponent's waist. Foundational guard with high submission threat.

nodes.name: Open Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player's legs are open and active but not locked. Transitional guard state with many sub-varieties.

nodes.name: Half Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player controls one of the top player's legs between their own. Classic transitional position.

nodes.name: Side Control
nodes.description: Top player lies perpendicular across bottom player's torso, controlling with hip and shoulder pressure.

nodes.name: North South
nodes.description: Top player faces opposite direction across bottom player's chest, controlling head and hips.

nodes.name: Mount
nodes.description: Top player straddles bottom player's torso sitting on their hips. High-percentage dominant position.

nodes.name: Technical Mount
nodes.description: S-mount variation where top player's leg is over opponent's arm, isolating the limb for attacks.

nodes.name: Back Control
nodes.description: Top player behind opponent with hooks in and seatbelt grip. Highest-value position in BJJ.

nodes.name: Turtle
nodes.description: Defender on all fours, protecting neck and back. Transitional defensive position.

nodes.name: Butterfly Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player uses both feet as hooks inside opponent's inner thighs. Mobile and sweeping guard.

nodes.name: Deep Half Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player entirely beneath opponent's body with full leg entanglement, head under the hips.

nodes.name: De La Riva Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player hooks one leg around the outside of opponent's lead leg with foot on hip. Long-range guard.

nodes.name: X-Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player under opponent with both hooks beneath opponent's hips, controlling both legs.

nodes.name: Knee on Belly
nodes.description: Top player's knee pressed on bottom player's abdomen with an upright base. Transitional pressure position.

nodes.name: Spider Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player controls both sleeves and places feet on opponent's biceps. Long-range grip-based guard.

nodes.name: Worm Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player feeds their lapel around the opponent's leg and grips it, creating a powerful entanglement. Pioneered by Keenan Cornelius. Gi only.

nodes.name: Lasso Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player threads their leg through the opponent's arm, wrapping around it to control the sleeve. Excellent for breaking posture and attacking. Gi only.

nodes.name: 50/50 Guard
nodes.description: Both players have equal leg entanglement with mirrored inside position. Primary position for modern heel hook exchanges and reaping attacks.

nodes.name: Rubber Guard
nodes.description: Bottom player controls their own leg high over opponent's shoulder using the hand, breaking posture completely. Requires flexibility. Associated with 10th Planet system.

nodes.name: Reverse De La Riva
nodes.description: Bottom player hooks inside the opponent's lead leg from behind, controlling the ankle with the opposite hand. Entry to back takes and leg locks.

nodes.name: Z-Guard
nodes.description: Half guard variation where the bottom player places their knee shield across the opponent's hip to block the crossface and create space. Also called knee shield half guard.

nodes.name: Ashi Garami
nodes.description: Single leg entanglement where bottom player controls the inside position on one leg with hooks, setting up heel hooks and ankle locks. Foundation of modern leg lock systems.

nodes.name: Dogfight
nodes.description: Both players on all fours facing each other with an underhook and overhook exchange. Critical transitional position from half guard.

nodes.name: Rear Naked Choke
nodes.description: Choking arm around opponent's neck, other arm bracing behind head. Primary submission from back control.

nodes.name: Triangle Choke
nodes.description: Legs triangled around opponent's neck and one arm, cutting off carotid arteries. Versatile strangle.

nodes.name: Armbar
nodes.description: Elbow joint hyperextended by controlling the arm and bridging hips. Available from multiple positions.

nodes.name: Omoplata
nodes.description: Shoulder joint locked by trapping opponent's arm with the legs and rotating the hip.

nodes.name: Kimura
nodes.description: Figure-four grip on opponent's arm with wrist bent behind the back. Available from many positions.

nodes.name: Americana
nodes.description: Figure-four wrist lock with arm bent at low angle on the mat. Most common from side control and mount.

nodes.name: Guillotine
nodes.description: Arm around opponent's neck applying pressure on windpipe or carotid arteries. Available standing and guard.

nodes.name: D'Arce Choke
nodes.description: Figure-four arm triangle choke with arm threaded under neck. Typically applied from top positions.

nodes.name: Bow and Arrow Choke
nodes.description: Collar choke with cross-collar grip and belt or leg control. Primary gi submission from back control.

nodes.name: Ankle Lock
nodes.description: Straight heel pressure on ankle joint using body as lever. Foot lock targeting the ankle ligaments.

nodes.name: Heel Hook
nodes.description: Rotational knee attack by controlling the heel and twisting. High-percentage leg lock in modern BJJ.

nodes.name: North-South Choke
nodes.description: Forearm or bicep pressed across the trachea from a north-south position while the head is trapped under the body. Requires precise shoulder placement.

nodes.name: Clock Choke
nodes.description: Collar choke applied from turtle position where the top player drives their hip into the side of the opponent's head while pulling the collar. Gi only.

nodes.name: Ezekiel Choke
nodes.description: Sleeve-assisted choke using one hand inside the sleeve to grip the other forearm, applying pressure across the neck. Works from mount and guard. Gi only.

nodes.name: Banana Split
nodes.description: Hip adductor stretch submission from leg entanglement where both legs are split in opposite directions using the body as a fulcrum. Applied from 50/50 or ashi garami.

nodes.name: Calf Slicer
nodes.description: Compression lock on the calf muscle by wedging the knee or forearm behind the opponent's knee joint. Applied from leg entanglements.

nodes.name: Peruvian Necktie
nodes.description: Twisting choke from front headlock or turtle where the top player uses a collar tie and leg over the back to apply a neck crank and blood choke simultaneously.

nodes.name: Baseball Bat Choke
nodes.description: Collar choke using two grips pointing the same direction, applied by rotating the forearms. Set up from side control or mount and finished by rolling through. Gi only.

nodes.name: Mounted Triangle
nodes.description: Triangle choke applied from the mount position by swinging a leg over the opponent's head and locking the triangle while maintaining the top position.

nodes.name: Gogoplata
nodes.description: Choke using the shin across the trachea from rubber guard or inverted guard. Bottom player threads the leg deep under the opponent's chin and applies downward pressure on the head.

nodes.name: Twister
nodes.description: Spinal lock from back control using leg hooks to rotate the bottom half of the opponent's body while turning the upper body the opposite direction. Associated with 10th Planet.

nodes.name: Kneebar
nodes.description: Hyperextension of the knee joint by wrapping the leg under the armpit and bridging the hips into the joint, similar mechanics to an armbar but targeting the knee.

nodes.name: Scissor Sweep
nodes.description: Bottom player scissors legs to topple opponent from closed guard using collar and sleeve grips.

nodes.name: Hip Bump Sweep
nodes.description: Sit-up sweep from closed guard using hip explosion to roll opponent over. Sets up kimura if blocked.

nodes.name: Butterfly Sweep
nodes.description: Lifting sweep using butterfly hooks and an underhook to toss opponent to the side.

nodes.name: X-Guard Sweep
nodes.description: Sweep from x-guard lifting opponent with both hooks and dumping them to the side.

nodes.name: Flower Sweep
nodes.description: Pendulum sweep from closed guard using cross-collar grip and ankle control to roll opponent.

nodes.name: Waiter Sweep
nodes.description: Sweep from deep half guard where the bottom player extends the trapped leg upward like a waiter holding a tray, rolling the opponent over.

nodes.name: Sickle Sweep
nodes.description: Hook sweep from De La Riva or butterfly guard using a sickle motion of the leg to knock out the opponent's base leg while pulling the sleeve.

nodes.name: Lasso Sweep
nodes.description: Sweep from lasso guard using the leg wrap and sleeve grip to rotate the opponent off-balance and roll them to the side.

nodes.name: Torreando Pass
nodes.description: Speed pass that controls both knees and moves the hips around the guard. Primarily from open guard.

nodes.name: Knee Slice Pass
nodes.description: Pass by threading knee between opponent's legs and using hip pressure to cut through.

nodes.name: Double Under Pass
nodes.description: Stacking pass by controlling both legs under the arms and passing with bodyweight.

nodes.name: Over Under Pass
nodes.description: Pass controlling one leg over the shoulder and one under, using hip pressure to clear guard.

nodes.name: Leg Drag Pass
nodes.description: Pass by dragging opponent's top leg across the body and establishing side control.

nodes.name: X-Pass
nodes.description: Fast pass that uses an X-step around the opponent's open guard while controlling the knee with one hand. Creates distance and angles around the guard quickly.

nodes.name: Cartwheel Pass
nodes.description: Dynamic passing technique that uses a cartwheel motion to spin around De La Riva or reverse De La Riva guard. Requires timing and athleticism.

nodes.name: Smash Pass
nodes.description: Pressure pass that smashes the opponent's legs flat to the mat using bodyweight and hip pressure, eliminating the guard frame before passing.

nodes.name: Long Step Pass
nodes.description: Pass that uses a long lateral step to clear the knee shield and establish a crossface. Most effective against z-guard and knee shield half guard.

nodes.name: Double Leg Takedown
nodes.description: Shooting to both legs and driving through opponent to bring them down. Fundamental wrestling takedown.

nodes.name: Single Leg Takedown
nodes.description: Securing one leg and using trips, lifts, or drives to take opponent down. Versatile wrestling takedown.

nodes.name: Judo Throw
nodes.description: Hip-based or shoulder-based throw using kuzushi, tsukuri, and kake. Covers ippon seoi nage, o-goshi, etc.

nodes.name: Hip Throw
nodes.description: Throw using hip insertion to lift and rotate the opponent off the mat. Includes o-goshi, harai goshi, and tsuri goshi variants. Requires body contact and kuzushi.

nodes.name: Foot Sweep
nodes.description: Sweeping the opponent's foot or ankle at the moment of weight transfer to knock them down. Timing-dependent technique requiring precise entry. Includes o-uchi gari and ko-uchi gari.

nodes.name: Fireman's Carry
nodes.description: Arm drag takedown where the attacker gets under the opponent and rolls them over the shoulders. Often used as a reaction to an overhook or arm reach.

nodes.name: Elbow-Knee Escape
nodes.description: Primary escape from mount using a frame with the elbow on the hip and knee shield to create space, then shrimping to recover half guard. Also called the bridge-and-elbow escape.

nodes.name: Shrimp Escape
nodes.description: Hip escape movement from side control or mount using alternating shrimping (hip extension) to create space and recover guard. Foundational defensive movement in BJJ.

nodes.name: Back Escape Roll
nodes.description: Escape from back control by turning into the choking arm, tucking the chin, and rolling through to face the opponent. Sacrifices position to avoid the choke.

nodes.name: Granby Roll
nodes.description: Shoulder roll escape from turtle or back control that inverts the defender and allows them to re-guard or come up to a seated position. Wrestler's technique adapted for BJJ.

nodes.name: Running Man Escape
nodes.description: Leg lock defense technique that uses a rolling motion to pop the heel out of single leg entanglements. Effective against straight ashi garami but risky against heel hooks.

nodes.name: Sprawl
nodes.description: Defensive reaction to a takedown shot by driving the hips down and back while pushing the opponent's head down. Counters double leg and single leg takedowns.

nodes.name: Posture Up
nodes.description: Guard passing defense where the bottom player breaks the opponent's posture by pulling the head or collar down. Also a top player defense to prevent sweep and submission setups.

nodes.name: Arm Tuck Defense
nodes.description: Defense against kimura and americana by pulling the arm tight to the body and framing before the grip is fully established. Prevents the figure-four lock from being secured.

nodes.name: Stack Defense
nodes.description: Counter to triangle choke and armbar from guard by stacking the opponent's hips over their head, removing their leverage. Allows the top player to posture up and escape.

nodes.name: Frame and Shrimp
nodes.description: Combined counter to guard passes using frames on the hip and shoulder to stall progress, then shrimping away to re-establish guard. The fundamental passing defense.

nodes.name: Kuzushi
nodes.description: The principle of unbalancing the opponent before executing a throw, sweep, or technique. Without kuzushi, most throws and sweeps will fail. The first stage of any throwing technique.

nodes.name: Hip Escape
nodes.description: The foundational defensive movement of BJJ — extending the hips away from the opponent by pushing off the foot and bridging the hips laterally. Creates space to recover guard or escape positions.

nodes.name: Base and Posture
nodes.description: Base is the wide stable foundation preventing sweeps. Posture is the upright spine alignment preventing chokes and breaks. Both must be maintained simultaneously in top and bottom positions.

nodes.name: Framing
nodes.description: Using the skeletal structure of the arms and legs as rigid frames against the opponent's body to create and maintain space. Frames buy time for escapes and guard recovery.

nodes.name: Grips
nodes.description: Grip selection and control is the first battle in BJJ. Controlling the opponent's sleeve, collar, wrist or leg dictates which techniques are available. Breaking grips is as important as establishing them.

nodes.name: Weight Distribution
nodes.description: Understanding where the opponent's weight is loaded determines when sweeps and takedowns are available. Techniques succeed when the opponent's weight is shifted away from the direction of the throw.

nodes.name: Bridge
nodes.description: Explosive upward hip extension from the bottom position using the feet and shoulders as base. Used to unbalance the top player and create space for escapes or rolls.

nodes.name: Marcelo Garcia
nodes.description: 5x ADCC champion and multiple gi world champion. Considered one of the greatest of all time. Known for his butterfly guard, arm drags, guillotine choke, and systematic back takes.

nodes.name: Gordon Ryan
nodes.description: Dominant no-gi competitor and multiple ADCC champion. Known for his systematic back taking, leg lock entries, and submission-only approach. Student of John Danaher.

nodes.name: Keenan Cornelius
nodes.description: Known for pioneering modern lapel guard systems including the worm guard. Multiple ADCC and gi world championship finalist with an innovative and technical game.

nodes.name: Roger Gracie
nodes.description: Widely considered the greatest gi competitor of all time. 10x world champion known for simple, fundamental techniques — particularly the cross-collar choke from every position.

nodes.name: Mikey Musumeci
nodes.description: Multiple gi and no-gi world champion. Known for his closed guard game, leg locks, and technical precision. Student of John Danaher.

nodes.name: Bernardo Faria
nodes.description: 5x gi world champion known for his deep half guard game and smothering over-under pass. Represents old-school fundamentals applied at the highest level.

nodes.name: John Danaher
nodes.description: Renowned coach and system developer who transformed modern BJJ. Developed the Danaher Death Squad's systematic approach to leg locks, back attacks, and front headlock systems.

nodes.name: Leandro Lo
nodes.description: Multiple gi world champion known for his dynamic athleticism, aggressive passing, knee on belly game, and ability to finish from any position.

nodes.name: Romulo Barral
nodes.description: 7x gi world champion known for his spider guard and De La Riva combinations, creative guard game, and dangerous kimura from top positions.

nodes.name: Andre Galvao
nodes.description: Multiple gi and no-gi world champion and ADCC champion. Well-rounded game with strong wrestling, guard passing, and submission hunting. Founder of Atos Jiu-Jitsu.

nodes.name: Marcelo's Butterfly Back System
nodes.description: Marcelo Garcia's game plan built around butterfly guard as the foundation for arm drags, underhooks, and taking the back. Finishes primarily with RNC and guillotine. Works in both gi and no-gi.

nodes.name: Keenan's Lapel Guard System
nodes.description: Keenan Cornelius's innovative gi system using lapel entanglements — primarily worm guard from De La Riva — to control opponents and attack with triangles, omoplatas, and back takes.

nodes.name: Roger's Closed Guard System
nodes.description: Roger Gracie's game plan centered on breaking posture from closed guard and finishing with the cross-collar choke or transitioning to mount for the same finish. Deceptively simple and brutally effective.

nodes.name: Bernardo's Half Guard System
nodes.description: Bernardo Faria's systematic use of deep half guard sweeps and the over-under pass. Built on smothering pressure and positional control rather than speed or athleticism.

nodes.name: Gordon Ryan's Back System
nodes.description: Gordon Ryan's systematic approach to taking and maintaining back control developed under John Danaher. Emphasizes seat belt control, hook placement, and choking mechanics.

nodes.name: DDS Leg Lock System
nodes.description: The Danaher Death Squad's revolutionary systematic approach to leg locks. Built around ashi garami entries, inside heel hook finishes, and 50/50 exchanges. Transformed modern no-gi competition.

nodes.name: Arm Drag
nodes.description: Gripping the opponent's wrist or elbow and pulling it across their body while stepping behind them to expose the back. Marcelo Garcia's signature entry from standing and butterfly guard.

nodes.name: Berimbolo
nodes.description: Inversion technique from De La Riva guard where the bottom player rolls under the opponent, inverting their hips to expose and take the back. Popularized by the Miyao brothers and Caio Terra.

nodes.name: Cross Collar Choke
nodes.description: Choke using two deep collar grips pulling in opposite directions to compress the carotid arteries. Roger Gracie's signature technique from closed guard and mount. Simple but devastating.

nodes.name: Loop Choke
nodes.description: Collar choke applied by looping the collar around the opponent's neck using rotation and forward pressure. Extremely effective from turtle or when the opponent is posturing up.

nodes.name: Snap Down
nodes.description: Pulling the opponent's head sharply downward using a collar tie or neck grip to break their posture and expose the back or neck. Common wrestling entry to guillotine and back takes.

nodes.name: Tripod Sweep
nodes.description: Open guard sweep using a sleeve grip, foot on the hip, and the other foot hooking the ankle. Creates a tripod effect to topple the standing opponent forward. One of the most reliable open guard sweeps.

nodes.name: Single Leg X-Guard
nodes.description: Leg entanglement controlling one leg with both hooks — one behind the knee, one on the hip. Primary entry to inside heel hooks and ankle locks in modern no-gi.

nodes.name: Sit-Up Guard
nodes.description: Guard variant where the bottom player sits up to an underhook position, similar to a wrestling stance. Creates opportunities for double legs, singles, arm drags, and back takes.

nodes.name: Body Lock Takedown
nodes.description: Controlling the opponent from behind by locking both hands around their waist and using lifts, trips, or drives to bring them down. Effective from the back clinch.

nodes.name: Lockdown
nodes.description: Half guard variation where the bottom player cross-locks their legs around the opponent's trapped leg in a figure-four, immobilizing it. Foundation of Eddie Bravo's half guard system.

nodes.name: Electric Chair
nodes.description: Hip adductor submission from lockdown where the bottom player obtains an underhook and applies a splitting force between the legs. Causes extreme discomfort and often leads to a sweep or back take.

nodes.name: Rear Triangle
nodes.description: Triangle choke applied from back control by threading one leg over the opponent's arm and under the neck, locking the triangle while maintaining the back position.

nodes.name: Crucifix
nodes.description: Position from turtle where the top player traps one arm between their legs and controls the other arm, leaving the opponent's neck fully exposed. Allows chokes and armbars with no defense available.
